content.full_text:
State of California

Memorandum

To      :  File                                    Date  :  Jan. 11, 1977


From  :  FAIR POLITICAL PRACTICES COMMISSION
         Ted Prim

Subject:  Letter from Edith Brewer.

I told her that currently there were no procedures for
terminating a committee.  However, if one were not re-
ceiving contributions or making expenditures one need not
file reports.  Thus, by terminating all activities one can
eliminate virtually all obligations under the Political Re-
form Act.  I also told her that we were contemplating a
regulation which would establish a termination procedure.

She actually seemed to be more interested in how a person
terminated his or relationship with a campaign committee.  I
indicated that pursuant to our Act, no procedure was neces-
sary except that with respect to treasurers and principal
officers, the Statement of Organization would have to be
amended by the committee.

I told her that if she or the person in question wished to
discuss this matter further, I would be happy to do so.


TP:jo

Office of the Secretary of State
March Long Lu

POLITICAL REFORM DIVISION
(916) 322-1880

15 Capitol Mall, Rm 200
P.O. Box 1467
Sacramento, California 95807

October 20, 1976


Verne K. Perryman
967 West G Street
Ontario, California 91762

Dear Mr. Perryman:

Thank you for your letter dated April 13, 1976 which advised our office that your committee, Committee to Elect Anthony "Tony" Rizzo, ID# 746680 has become inactive.

The delay in responding to your request was due to the fact that the Political Reform Act contains no provisions by which a committee may terminate; nor has the Fair Political Practices Commission issued any regulations in this area. Your statement of organization, together with your letter, will be placed in an inactive file. If your committee receives contributions or makes expenditures, please immediately notify our office that your committee has again become active.

Please be aware that regulations or legislation may come into effect which would influence your committee status. Further questions pertaining to the status of your committee should be directed to the Technical Assistance and Analysis Division of the Fair Political Practices Commission at (916) 322-5660.

If our office may be of any assistance to you, do not hesitate to contact the undersigned.

Sincerely,

WILLIAM N. DURLEY
Assistant to the Secretary of State
Elections and Political Reform

Marge Nunes
MARGE NUNES
Political Reform Division

WND/MN:cb

Y TO
LATIVE ADDRESS
P.O. BOX 1956a
SACRAMENTO, CA 95814
(916) 445-6868

COMMITTEES
AGRICULTURE AND
WATER RESOURCES
LOCAL GOVERNMENT
NATURAL RESOURCES AND
WILDLIFE, VICE CHAIRMAN
REVENUE AND TAXATION

DISTRICT OFFICE ADDRESSES
250 WEST FIRST STREET
SAN BERNARDINO, CA 92401
(714) 884-2165

1063 WEST 6TH STREET
SUITE 102
ONTARIO, CALIFORNIA 91716
(714) 983-3566

STATE SENATOR
RUBEN S. AYALA
THIRTY-SECOND SENATORIAL DISTRICT

California State Senate

December 28, 1976


Dr. Daniel H. Lowenstein, Chairman
Fair Political Practices Commission
Post Office Box 807
Sacramento, California 95814

Dear Dr. Lowenstein:

     Senator Ayala asked that I send the enclosed letter
from his constituent Verne K. Perryman, to you. I talked with
Legislative Counsel and they advised me that the Commission
is in the process of preparing regulations to change the provisions
to terminate a committee. Possibly you could inform us on this.

     The Senator was thinking of introducing legislation to
do this, however if you are already doing it there is no use in
us getting involved.

     We would appreciate hearing from you. Thanks.

                                        Sincerely,

                                        [signature]
                                        Edith Brewer
                                        Administrative Assistant to
                                        Senator Ruben S. Ayala

Enclosure

sections.conclusion: Your statement of organization, together with your letter, will be placed in an inactive file. If your committee receives contributions or makes expenditures, please immediately notify our office that your committee has again become active. Please be aware that regulations or legislation may come into effect which would influence your committee status.
sections.question_synthetic: How can a campaign committee be terminated or discontinued, and what procedures and filing obligations apply when a committee becomes inactive?
sections.conclusion_synthetic: The FPPC placed the inactive committee in an inactive file with no formal termination procedure currently available; however, committees that cease all activities and don't receive contributions or make expenditures have no reporting obligations under the Political Reform Act.
sections.parsing_notes: No section headers found; LLM: Document contains multiple related items: internal FPPC memorandum from Ted Prim, formal FPPC response letter to Verne Perryman from William N. Durley, and transmittal letter from State Senator Ruben Ayala's office. Core advice letter is the formal response dated October 20, 1976. Some text quality issues with OCR artifacts in the Senator's letter.

embedding.summary: This advice letter from the FPPC responds to inquiries about committee termination procedures, clarifying that no formal termination provisions exist in the Political Reform Act but that inactive committees with no financial activity have minimal reporting obligations.
